user.title: Vesta Configuration
user.description: Choose the type of configuration you want to add.

global.title: Global Home Settings
global.description: These defaults apply to all rooms unless individually overridden.

data_description.comfort_temp: Target temperature when someone is home and the schedule is active.
data_description.eco_temp: Target temperature during inactive schedule periods (e.g. overnight).
data_description.away_temp: Target temperature when everyone is away from home.
data_description.avg_travel_speed: Used to estimate arrival time for smart pre-heating. Set to your typical commute speed.
data_description.presence_sensors: Person entities used to detect if someone is home. Drives automatic home/away switching.
data_description.schedule_entity: A Schedule helper that controls when comfort vs eco temperatures apply. ON = comfort, OFF = eco.
data_description.weather_entity: Used for weather compensation: boosts heating when it's very cold outside.
data_description.override_switch: An input_boolean or switch that, when turned on, forces all heaters to maximum heat. Use for emergency cold situations.
data_description.vacation_entity: Link an input_boolean or binary_sensor to control vacation mode dynamically. When ON, all rooms drop to anti-frost temperature. Takes priority over the static toggle below.
data_description.vacation_state: Static fallback: enable if no vacation entity is configured above.
data_description.boiler_entity: The boiler entity Vesta should control directly. When any room calls for heat, Vesta turns the boiler on and sets its flow temperature. Leave empty if you manage the boiler with a separate automation.
data_description.boiler_offset: Flow temperature offset above the highest active room setpoint. For example, if a room targets 21°C and the offset is 5°C, the boiler is set to 26°C.
data_description.energy_price_kwh: Your current electricity rate. Used to estimate monthly savings in euros.
data_description.energy_kwh_this_year: Total heating energy consumed this year. Used to estimate kWh and cost savings per room. You can update this each year.

area.title: Select Room Area
area.description: Select a Home Assistant area to auto-discover heaters and sensors. Skip this step to configure entities manually.

data_description.area: Entities assigned to this area will be automatically detected and pre-selected.

room.title: Room Configuration
room.description: Configure the heaters and sensors for this room.

data_description.name: Display name for this climate entity (e.g. 'Vesta Living Room').
data_description.heaters: Select the TRVs, switches, or climate entities that control the heating in this room.
data_description.target_sensor: One or more temperature sensors. Multiple readings are averaged. If all go offline, the TRV's internal sensor is used as fallback.
data_description.window_sensor: When this sensor detects an open window, heating is paused to save energy.
data_description.window_delay: Minutes to wait after a window opens before suppressing heating. Useful for underfloor heating where brief ventilation should not interrupt a long heating cycle. Set to 0 to suppress immediately.

overrides.name: Room-Specific Overrides
overrides.description: Enable a toggle only if this room should use different settings than the global defaults.

data_description.override_presence: When enabled, this room uses its own presence sensors instead of the global ones.
data_description.presence_sensors: Person entities that determine if someone is in or near this room.
data_description.override_schedule: When enabled, this room follows its own heating schedule.
data_description.schedule_entity: A Schedule helper specific to this room. ON = comfort, OFF = eco.
data_description.override_weather: When enabled, this room uses a different weather source for compensation.
data_description.weather_entity: Weather entity used for outdoor temperature compensation in this room.
data_description.override_comfort: When enabled, this room uses its own comfort temperature instead of the global default.
data_description.comfort_temp: Target temperature when this room's schedule is active and someone is home.
data_description.override_away: When enabled, this room uses its own away temperature instead of the global default.
data_description.away_temp: Target temperature for this room when everyone is away.

error.missing_presence_sensors: You must select at least one presence sensor when the override is enabled.
error.missing_schedule_entity: You must select a schedule entity when the override is enabled.
error.missing_weather_entity: You must select a weather provider when the override is enabled.

data_description.comfort_temp: Target temperature when someone is home and the schedule is active.
data_description.eco_temp: Target temperature during inactive schedule periods (e.g. overnight).
data_description.away_temp: Target temperature when everyone is away from home.
data_description.avg_travel_speed: Used to estimate arrival time for smart pre-heating.
data_description.presence_sensors: Person entities used to detect if someone is home.
data_description.schedule_entity: Schedule helper: ON = comfort, OFF = eco.
data_description.weather_entity: Used for weather compensation when it's very cold outside.
data_description.override_switch: An input_boolean or switch that, when turned on, forces all heaters to maximum heat. Use for emergency cold situations.
data_description.vacation_entity: Link an input_boolean or binary_sensor to control vacation mode dynamically. When ON, all rooms drop to anti-frost temperature. Takes priority over the static toggle below.
data_description.vacation_state: Static fallback: enable if no vacation entity is configured above.
data_description.boiler_entity: The boiler entity Vesta should control directly. When any room calls for heat, Vesta turns the boiler on and sets its flow temperature. Leave empty if you manage the boiler separately.
data_description.boiler_offset: Flow temperature offset above the highest active room setpoint.
data_description.energy_price_kwh: Your current electricity rate. Used to estimate monthly savings in euros.
data_description.energy_kwh_this_year: Total heating energy consumed this year. Update this each year to keep estimates accurate.

data_description.name: Display name for this climate entity.
data_description.heaters: TRVs, switches, or climate entities that control this room's heating.
data_description.target_sensor: One or more temperature sensors. Multiple readings are averaged. If all go offline, the TRV's internal sensor is used as fallback.
data_description.window_sensor: Heating pauses when this sensor detects an open window.
data_description.window_delay: Minutes to wait after a window opens before suppressing heating. Useful for underfloor heating. Set to 0 to suppress immediately.

overrides.name: Room-Specific Overrides
overrides.description: Enable a toggle only if this room should use different settings than the global defaults.

data_description.override_presence: Use room-specific presence sensors instead of global.
data_description.presence_sensors: Person entities for this room's presence detection.
data_description.schedule_entity: Schedule helper for this room. ON = comfort, OFF = eco.
data_description.override_weather: Use a different weather source for this room.
data_description.weather_entity: Weather entity for outdoor temperature compensation.
data_description.override_comfort: Use a custom comfort temperature for this room.
data_description.comfort_temp: Comfort temperature when schedule is active.
data_description.override_away: Use a custom away temperature for this room.
data_description.away_temp: Away temperature when no one is home.

error.missing_presence_sensors: You must select at least one presence sensor when the override is enabled.
error.missing_schedule_entity: You must select a schedule entity when the override is enabled.
error.missing_weather_entity: You must select a weather provider when the override is enabled.
